km.Wales: វេលស៍, uណៃteត គីnហ្គដុម

zh.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrot於1272年出生在英國威爾斯。他於1300年與Jane Verch John在英國威爾斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1300年在他的家鄉享年28歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrot于1272年出生在英国威尔斯。他于1300年与Jane Verch John在英国威尔斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1300年在他的家乡享年28岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Geza, Jeno, Arpad, Attila, Csaba.

Hungarian: variant of   Papp  , meaning ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’. This surname is also found in Romania, Slovakia, Croatia, and Serbia, and also among Rusyns (in Serbia and Croatia). See also   Pop   2 and   Popa  .
English: variant of   Pape   1.

variants.name: Papp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Zoltan, Geza, Istvan, Gabor, Miklos, Tibor, Arpad, Denes, Imre, Attila.
German (Bavaria, Tyrol): from Middle High German pap ‘baby food’, Dutch pap ‘porridge, pap’, hence perhaps a nickname denoting someone who had lost his teeth or subsisted on a meager diet.

Hungarian: from pap ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’, probably an occupational name for someone in the service of a priest or a nickname for a pious person. This surname is also found in Romania and Slovakia, and also among Rusyns. Compare Pap and Pop .

nameMeanings.name: Steven
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish, English, Dutch, and North German: from the personal name Steven, from Latin Stephanus, Greek Stephanos ‘crown’. This was a popular name throughout Christendom in the Middle Ages, having been borne by the first Christian martyr, stoned to death at Jerusalem three years after the death of Christ. In North America, this surname has also absorbed various cognates from other languages, e.g. German Steffen , German and Polish Stefan , Slovenian and Slovak Štefan, Hungarian István (see Istvan ), and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see Stevens ).

variants.name: Stefanic
meanings: Slovenian and Slovak (Štefanič); Croatian (Štefanić): patronymic from the personal name Štefan, from Latin Stephanus (see Steven ).

variants.name: Schoepke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ewald, Ilse, Otto.
Germanized form (Schöpke) of Sorbian Šćepk, Šćěpk, and Šćěpka: from a pet form of the personal name Šćěpan, Šćepan, from Latin Stephanus (see Steven ).

variants.name: Stec
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Stanislaw, Jacek, Aniela, Augustyn, Boguslaw, Boleslaw, Casimir, Genowefa, Halina, Janina, Jerzy, Myroslaw.

Polish and Rusyn (from Poland); Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) (Štec): from the personal names Stec, Štec, pet forms of Stefan, Štefan, vernacular forms of Latin Stephanus (see Steven ). Compare Steck 3 and Stetz 1.

variants.name: Steffen
meanings: North German and Dutch: from the personal name Steffen, from Latin Stephanus (see Steven ).

variants.name: Stefan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Franz, Kurt, Helmut, Darrold, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinrich, Heinz, Otto.  Romanian Constantin, Gheorghe, Ilie, Nicolae, Gelu, Mihai, Mircea, Radu, Todor, Vasile, Viorel.

Romanian and Polish; Slovenian, Croatian, and Slovak (Štefan): from the personal names Stefan (Romanian, Polish) and Štefan (Slovenian, Croatian dialect, Slovak), from Greek Stephanos, Latin Stephanus (see Steven ).

American shortened form of various patronymics and other derivatives from the personal names Štefan and Stefan(os) ‘Steven’, e.g. Slovenian Štefančič and Croatian Štefančić (see Stefancic ), Slovak Štefanovič (see Stefanovic ), and Greek Stefanopoulos . Compare Stephan and Steven .

variants.name: Stefanko
meanings: Slovak and Croatian (Štefanko): from a pet form of the personal name Štefan, Latin Stephanus (see Steven ).

variants.name: Stecz
meanings: Polish and Rusyn (from Poland): from the personal name Stecz, a pet form of Stefan, from Latin Stephanus (see Steven ). Compare Stech 2.

variants.name: Pesta
meanings: Hungarian: from a pet form of the personal name István, Hungarian form of Steven .

variants.name: Stefanidis
meanings: Greek: patronymic from the personal name Stefanos (see Steven ). The suffix -idis, meaning ‘son/descendant of’, is a variant of the classical -ides which was revived in the 19th century, in particular by Greeks from Asia Minor and Eastern Thrace.

variants.name: Stech
meanings: Polish and Rusyn (from Poland); Czech (Štěch) and Slovak (Štech): from the personal name Stech, Štěch, Štech, pet forms of Polish Stefan, Czech Štěpán, Slovak Štefan, equivalents of Latin Stephanus (see Steven ).
Americanized form of Polish and Rusyn (from Poland)   Stecz  , a cognate of 1 above.
East German: from a pet form of   Steffen  .

nameMeanings.name: Perrot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Gilles, Veronique.
French: from a pet form of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Perrotte .
Breton: from a French-influenced pet form (see 1 above) of the personal name Per, Breton form of Peter . Compare Perro .

variants.name: Pierre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Antoine, Yves, Michel, Jean Claude, Mireille, Jean Robert, Francois, Monique, Raoul, Serge.
French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian:

from the personal name Pierre, French form of Peter (see also below). This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Jean and Joseph ) and in Saint Martin. It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in Trinidad and Tobago, Saint Lucia, Grenada, and Bahamas).

variants.name: Perrotte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Damien, Henri.
Altered form of French Perrot or Perrault , reflecting the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.
French: from the feminine form of   Perrot  .

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

en.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrot was born in 1272, in Wales, United Kingdom. He married Jane Verch John in 1300, in Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1300, in his hometown, at the age of 28.

fr.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrot est né en 1272, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Jane Verch John en 1300, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1300, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 28 ans.

de.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrot wurde 1272 in Wales, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat 1300 in Wales, United Kingdom Jane Verch John geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1300 im Alter von 28 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrot è nato il nel 1272 nel Galles, Regno Unito. Lui sposato Jane Verch John nel 1300, nel Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1300, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 28 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrotは1272年にイギリスウェールズで生まれた。1300年にイギリスウェールズでJane Verch Johnと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1300年に故郷で28歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrot(은)는 1272년 영국 웨일스에서 태어났다. 그는 1300년 영국 웨일스에서 Jane Verch John(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1300년 그의 고향에서 28세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrot nasceu em 1272, em Gales, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com Jane Verch John em 1300, em Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1300, em sua cidade natal, com 28 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrot родился в 1272 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Verch John в 1300 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1300 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 28 лет.

es.lifeSummary: John Ap Steven Perrot nació en 1272, en Gales, Reino Unido. Se casó con Jane Verch John en 1300, en Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1300, en su pueblo, a la edad de 28 años.
